iterations.theme: Naslijeđe komunizma i UDBA-e u Hrvatskoj: Povijest, politika i borba za istinu

sections.subtitle: General Željko Glasnović: Neumorni borac protiv komunističkih ostataka
sections.screenshot_description: Voditelj predstavlja generala Željka Glasnovića, ističući njegovu vojnu karijeru i politički angažman.
sections.content: Voditelj predstavlja generala Željka Glasnovića kao hrvatskog ratnog zapovjednika i dragovoljca Domovinskog rata, ističući ga kao jednog od najiskrenijih i najdosljednijih glasova hrvatske državotvorne misli. Glasnović je rođen u Zagrebu, odrastao u Kanadi, a svoje vojno iskustvo stekao je u Legiji stranaca i Zaljevskom ratu. Godine 1991. vratio se u domovinu kako bi se borio u obrani Hrvatske te Bosne i Hercegovine, gdje je bio teško ranjen u bitci za Kupres. Njegova borba nastavila se i u politici te javnom prostoru, gdje se neprestano suprotstavlja komunističkim ostacima, lažnim narativima i selektivnom sjećanju. Poznat je po oštrim kritikama "duboke države", udbačkog kontinuiteta i relativizacije hrvatskih žrtava, te je u više navrata upozoravao na štetu koju Hrvatskoj nanosi neprovođenje lustracije. General Glasnović zahvaljuje na pozivu i pozdravlja sve pripadnike Hrvatskih snaga, HVO-a i HV-a.
sections.subtitle_en: General Željko Glasnović: Untiring fighter against communist remnants
sections.screenshot_description_en: The host introduces General Željko Glasnović, highlighting his military career and political engagement.
sections.content_en: The host introduces General Željko Glasnović as a Croatian war commander and volunteer of the Homeland War, highlighting him as one of the most sincere and consistent voices of Croatian state-building thought. Glasnović was born in Zagreb, grew up in Canada, and gained his military experience in the Foreign Legion and the Gulf War. In 1991, he returned to his homeland to fight in the defense of Croatia and Bosnia and Herzegovina, where he was severely wounded in the Battle of Kupres. His fight continued in politics and the public sphere, where he constantly opposes communist remnants, false narratives, and selective memory. He is known for sharp criticism of the "deep state," UDBA continuity, and the relativization of Croatian victims, and has repeatedly warned about the damage caused to Croatia by the failure to implement lustration. General Glasnović thanks for the invitation and greets all members of the Croatian Forces, HVO, and HV.

sections.subtitle: Dan bijelih traka: Podsjetnik na genocidnost i povijesne paralele
sections.screenshot_description: General Glasnović govori o Danu bijelih traka i povijesnom kontekstu srpskih zločina.
sections.content: Voditelj podsjeća na nedavnu obljetnicu Dana bijelih traka u Prijedoru, 31. svibnja 1992. godine, kada su Srbi, nakon zauzimanja tog područja, naredili svim nesrpskim stanovnicima (Hrvatima i Muslimanima) da nose bijele trake i na svoje kuće stave bijele plahte. General Glasnović ističe da to nije bio izoliran slučaj obilježavanja Hrvata te da je to bila najava genocidnosti koja se kasnije iskusila u Hrvatskoj i Bosni i Hercegovini. Naglašava važnost razumijevanja povijesti kako bi se shvatile posljedice i uzroci današnjih problema.
sections.subtitle_en: Day of White Ribbons: Reminder of genocidal nature and historical parallels
sections.screenshot_description_en: General Glasnović speaks about the Day of White Armbands and the historical context of Serbian crimes.
sections.content_en: The host recalls the recent anniversary of the Day of White Ribbons in Prijedor, May 31, 1992, when Serbs, after occupying that area, ordered all non-Serb residents (Croats and Muslims) to wear white ribbons and place white sheets on their houses. General Glasnović points out that this was not an isolated case of marking Croats and that it was an announcement of the genocidal nature that was later experienced in Croatia and Bosnia and Herzegovina. He emphasizes the importance of understanding history in order to understand the consequences and causes of today's problems.

sections.subtitle: Boljševički sotonizam: Zločini, masovne grobnice i kanibalizam kao povijesna opomena
sections.screenshot_description: General Glasnović govori o stravičnim komunističkim zločinima i masovnim grobnicama.
sections.content: General Glasnović duboko zaranja u povijesni kontekst, opisujući suživot s "cicarskim plemenima" koji su, prema njegovim riječima, poprimili poganske običaje. Spominje riječ "lapot" (senicid), praksu ubijanja starih osoba, što smatra nekršćanskim običajem. Ističe da se divljaštvo ponavljalo u ratu 90-ih, gdje je 90% ratnih zločina počinjeno od strane "ovih preko Drine i njihovih proksija". Navodi primjere poput sela Briševo i logora za silovanje, gdje su sustavno silovane žene i djevojčice. Povlači paralelu s Drugim svjetskim ratom, spominjući masovna silovanja, poput onih na Drini i u Sloveniji 1945. godine, gdje su "oni koji su zamijenili kokardu za petokraku" počinili stravične zločine. Posebno potresno opisuje slučaj Grozde Budak i Frede Pavlić, gdje je došlo do kanibalizma, što naziva "boljševičkim sotonizmom". Naglašava da je petokraka "sotonski znak" te da su oni koji su je nosili bili "mega ubojice". Voditelj dodaje da se i danas pronalaze masovne grobnice u Zagrebu, a političke stranke poput Možemo i SDP-a brane te zločine. General Glasnović to objašnjava kao posljedicu "boljševičkog sotonizma" i "pranja mozga" lažima. Ističe da nitko u Hrvatskoj nije osuđen za te zločine, a da su počinitelji živjeli u otetim stanovima. Govori o "aristocidu" (ubijanju intelektualaca, svećenika) i "democidu" (ubijanju mladih muškaraca), navodeći primjer Teznog kod Maribora gdje je ubijeno do 30.000 hrvatskih mladića. Zaključuje da su komunisti nanijeli više štete u pet godina nakon rata nego Osmanlije u pet stoljeća, te da je najgore "memoricid" – brisanje kolektivne memorije.
sections.subtitle_en: Bolshevik Satanism: Crimes, mass graves and cannibalism as a historical warning
sections.screenshot_description_en: General Glasnović speaks about horrific communist crimes and mass graves.
sections.content_en: General Glasnović deeply delves into the historical context, describing coexistence with "Cicarian tribes" who, according to his words, adopted pagan customs. He mentions the word "lapot" (senicide), the practice of killing old people, which he considers an un-Christian custom. He points out that savagery was repeated in the war of the 90s, where 90% of war crimes were committed by "those across the Drina and their proxies". He cites examples such as the village of Briševo and rape camps, where women and girls were systematically raped. He draws a parallel with the Second World War, mentioning mass rapes, such as those on the Drina and in Slovenia in 1945, where "those who exchanged the cockade for the five-pointed star" committed horrific crimes. He particularly movingly describes the case of Grozda Budak and Freda Pavlić, where cannibalism occurred, which he calls "Bolshevik Satanism". He emphasizes that the five-pointed star is a "satanic sign" and that those who wore it were "mega murderers". The host adds that mass graves are still being found in Zagreb today, and political parties like Možemo and SDP defend these crimes. General Glasnović explains this as a consequence of "Bolshevik Satanism" and "brainwashing" with lies. He points out that no one in Croatia has been convicted for these crimes, and that the perpetrators lived in stolen apartments. He speaks of "aristocide" (killing of intellectuals, priests) and "democide" (killing of young men), citing the example of Tezno near Maribor where up to 30,000 Croatian young men were killed. He concludes that communists inflicted more damage in five years after the war than the Ottomans in five centuries, and that the worst is "memoricide" – the erasure of collective memory.

sections.subtitle: Komunističko naslijeđe u Saboru: Samoopstojnost, pljačka imovine i uništenje katastra
sections.screenshot_description: General Glasnović objašnjava kako su komunističke strukture utjecale na hrvatsku politiku.
sections.content: Voditelj postavlja pitanje zašto se tek sada otkrivaju masovne grobnice i zločini, sugerirajući da je to povezano s umiranjem generacije političara koji su štitili svoje pretke. General Glasnović objašnjava da je na prvom zasjedanju Hrvatskog državnog sabora čak 80% zastupnika bilo povezano s Komunističkom partijom, OZNA-om ili UDBA-om. Ti su ljudi, prema njegovim riječima, prihvatili hrvatsku državu iz čistog samointeresa, kako bi sačuvali svoje privilegije i izbjegli sudbinu svojih drugova u Beogradu. Ističe da su ti kadrovi umirali i 90-ih godina, navodeći primjer šefa OZNA-e iz Bjelovara koji je doživio 102 godine i živio u vili na Tuškancu, dok su u Bjelovaru ubijane cijele obitelji. General Glasnović naglašava da se ne radi samo o masovnim ubojstvima, već i o "najvećoj pljački privatne imovine" izvan Sovjetskog Saveza. Zakon o povratu oduzete imovine nikada nije proveden, a spaljivanje zemljišnih knjiga u Lici onemogućilo je povratak ljudi i dovelo do demografske katastrofe. Opisuje kako su "opasci s krunašima" ušli u Zagreb, pobili starogradske obitelji i uselili se u njihove stanove, a njihovi potomci i danas žive u tim nekretninama. Ističe da se o tim zločinima šutjelo zbog straha od OZNA-e i UDBA-e, te da Bosna i Hercegovina još uvijek nije istražena po pitanju masovnih grobnica.
sections.subtitle_en: Communist legacy in the Parliament: Self-sustainability, plunder of property and destruction of the cadastre
sections.screenshot_description_en: General Glasnović explains how communist structures influenced Croatian politics.
sections.content_en: The host asks the question why mass graves and crimes are only now being discovered, suggesting that this is connected with the dying out of a generation of politicians who protected their ancestors. General Glasnović explains that at the first session of the Croatian State Parliament, as many as 80% of representatives were connected with the Communist Party, OZNA, or UDBA. These people, according to him, accepted the Croatian state out of pure self-interest, in order to preserve their privileges and avoid the fate of their comrades in Belgrade. He points out that these cadres were dying even in the 90s, citing the example of the head of OZNA from Bjelovar who lived to be 102 years old and lived in a villa in Tuškanac, while entire families were being killed in Bjelovar. General Glasnović emphasizes that it is not only about mass murders, but also about "the greatest robbery of private property" outside the Soviet Union. The Law on the Return of Confiscated Property was never implemented, and the burning of land registers in Lika prevented the return of people and led to a demographic catastrophe. He describes how "scoundrels with crown-wearers" entered Zagreb, killed old-town families and moved into their apartments, and their descendants still live in those properties today. He points out that these crimes were silenced due to fear of OZNA and UDBA, and that Bosnia and Herzegovina has still not been investigated regarding mass graves.

sections.subtitle: Udbaška mreža i skandal Marte Kos: Dokaz kontinuiranog utjecaja i izostanka lustracije
sections.screenshot_description: General Glasnović drži dosje UDBA-e i govori o skandalu Marte Kos.
sections.content: Voditelj pokazuje dokument – dosje UDBA-e o fizičkom radniku u Njemačkoj koji je dojavljivao o Hrvatima, naglašavajući mikro razinu do koje je išla udbaška mreža. Skandal s Martom Kos u Europskoj komisiji ponovno je bacio svjetlo na ovu problematiku. Slovenski premijer Janez Janša potvrdio je da su objavljeni dokumenti koji dokazuju da je Marta Kos bila infiltrirana kao novinarka u Deutsche Welle od strane jugoslavenskih službi (OZNA-e) radi prikupljanja podataka i širenja dezinformacija. Unatoč njezinim poricanjima, dokazi su objavljeni. General Glasnović ističe da se te strukture bore protiv kadrovske i akademske lustracije. Spominje da je UDBA ubila više Hrvata u dijaspori nego ukupno u domovini. Navodi primjer Siniše Glavaševića, čijeg je oca ubio srpski kriminalac Giška u tiskari u Münchenu, kako bi se prikrilo njegovo znanje o udbaškim suradnicima. General Glasnović ponavlja da je najveća pljačka privatne imovine izvan Sovjetskog Saveza izvršena u Jugoslaviji, a zakon o povratu nikada nije proveden. Voditelj dodaje da je privatizacija nastavila davati državnu imovinu u ruke kadrova bliskih vlasti, suprotno interesima naroda. General Glasnović ističe ironiju da su Hrvati iz dijaspore slali novac koji su "četnici i jugozomboidi" koristili za naoružanje protiv njih, a dijaspora je bila ključan faktor obrane Hrvatske.
sections.subtitle_en: UDBA network and Marta Kos's scandal: Proof of continuous influence and absence of lustration
sections.screenshot_description_en: General Glasnović holds the UDBA file and speaks about the scandal of Marte Kos.
sections.content_en: The host shows a document – an UDBA file about a physical worker in Germany who reported on Croats, emphasizing the micro-level to which the UDBA network extended. The scandal with Marta Kos in the European Commission again shed light on this issue. Slovenian Prime Minister Janez Janša confirmed that documents were published proving that Marta Kos was infiltrated as a journalist in Deutsche Welle by Yugoslav services (OZNA) for collecting data and spreading disinformation. Despite her denials, the evidence was published. General Glasnović points out that these structures fight against personnel and academic lustration. He mentions that UDBA killed more Croats in the diaspora than in total in the homeland. He cites the example of Siniša Glavašević, whose father was killed by the Serbian criminal Giška in a printing house in Munich, to cover up his knowledge of UDBA collaborators. General Glasnović reiterates that the largest robbery of private property outside the Soviet Union was carried out in Yugoslavia, and the law on restitution was never implemented. The host adds that privatization continued to give state property into the hands of cadres close to the authorities, contrary to the interests of the people. General Glasnović points out the irony that Croats from the diaspora sent money which "Chetniks and Yugozomboids" used for arming against them, and the diaspora was a key factor in the defense of Croatia.

sections.subtitle: Izborni inženjering i financiranje anti-hrvatskih medija: Sabotaža demokracije i nacionalnih interesa
sections.screenshot_description: General Glasnović govori o izbornom inženjeringu i financiranju medija.
sections.content: General Glasnović kritizira izborni inženjering u Hrvatskoj, ističući da tri zastupnika za milijune Hrvata izvan domovine, u usporedbi s dvanaest zajamčenih mjesta za manjine (Srbe, Mađare, Talijane), predstavljaju sabotažu. Voditelj naglašava da manjinski zastupnici ulaze u Sabor s minimalnim brojem glasova, što omogućuje vladajućima da ih koriste za formiranje Vlade. General Glasnović oštro kritizira financiranje "Srpskih novosti" s 2,5 milijuna kuna iz državnog proračuna, kao i drugih nevladinih udruga koje proizvode "anacionalne sadržaje" i "sjede na državnoj grbači". Predlaže ukidanje takvog financiranja i da se umjetnost financira privatnim sredstvima. Spominje i kritiku branitelja na filmski festival u Šibeniku zbog financiranja sadržaja koji vrijeđaju nacionalne vrijednosti.
sections.subtitle_en: Electoral engineering and financing of anti-Croatian media: Sabotage of democracy and national interests
sections.screenshot_description_en: General Glasnović speaks about electoral engineering and media financing.
sections.content_en: General Glasnović criticizes electoral engineering in Croatia, pointing out that three representatives for millions of Croats outside the homeland, compared to twelve guaranteed seats for minorities (Serbs, Hungarians, Italians), represent sabotage. The host emphasizes that minority representatives enter the Parliament with a minimal number of votes, which allows the ruling powers to use them for forming the Government. General Glasnović sharply criticizes the financing of "Srpske novosti" with 2.5 million kuna from the state budget, as well as other non-governmental organizations that produce "anti-national content" and "sit on the state's back." He proposes the abolition of such financing and that art be financed by private means. He also mentions the criticism of veterans regarding the film festival in Šibenik due to the financing of content that offends national values.

sections.subtitle: Neisplaćena ratna odšteta i revizionistička propaganda: Borba za istinu i nacionalno sjećanje
sections.screenshot_description: General Glasnović govori o ratnoj odšteti i povijesnom revizionizmu.
sections.content: General Glasnović ističe da Hrvatskoj nikada nije isplaćena ratna odšteta, procjenjujući izravnu štetu na 110 milijardi dolara. Spominje spaljene crkve iz srednjeg vijeka i 17. stoljeća, čije su vrijednosti odnesene u Beograd. Kritizira neprovođenje Ugovora o sukcesiji bivše Jugoslavije, gdje Hrvatska nije dobila svoj dio ambasada i konzulata. Umjesto da dobiva novac, Hrvatska i dalje financira one koji su joj nanijeli štetu. Voditelj i Glasnović se slažu da je to zastrašujuće. General Glasnović izražava optimizam, vjerujući da je povijest proces koji se odvija u ciklusima. Kritizira nedostatak vojnog muzeja u Hrvatskoj, unatoč bogatoj vojnoj povijesti, te spominje plakat u pariškom muzeju Les Invalides koji hvali hrvatske postrojbe u Napoleonovoj vojsci. Voditelj ističe da je to nastavak komunističke priče o Srbima kao ratnicima i Hrvatima kao zločincima, te da se povijest krivotvorila desetljećima. General Glasnović navodi primjer knjige "Hrvatski asovi II. Svjetskog rata" koja je tiskana u Vojno-istorijskom institutu u Beogradu, a na zadnjoj stranici sadrži revizionističke tvrdnje o Jasenovcu. Zaključuje da je to stari sovjetski sustav propagande: 10% istine zamotano u 90% laži.
sections.subtitle_en: Unpaid war reparations and revisionist propaganda: The struggle for truth and national memory
sections.screenshot_description_en: General Glasnović speaks about war reparations and historical revisionism.
sections.content_en: General Glasnović points out that Croatia has never been paid war reparations, estimating the direct damage at 110 billion dollars. He mentions burned churches from the Middle Ages and the 17th century, whose valuables were taken to Belgrade. He criticizes the non-implementation of the Agreement on Succession of the former Yugoslavia, where Croatia did not receive its share of embassies and consulates. Instead of receiving money, Croatia continues to finance those who caused it damage. The host and Glasnović agree that this is terrifying. General Glasnović expresses optimism, believing that history is a process that unfolds in cycles. He criticizes the lack of a military museum in Croatia, despite its rich military history, and mentions a poster in the Parisian museum Les Invalides that praises Croatian units in Napoleon's army. The host points out that this is a continuation of the communist narrative about Serbs as warriors and Croats as criminals, and that history has been falsified for decades. General Glasnović cites the example of the book "Hrvatski asovi II. Svjetskog rata" which was printed at the Military History Institute in Belgrade, and on the last page contains revisionist claims about Jasenovac. He concludes that this is the old Soviet system of propaganda: 10% truth wrapped in 90% lies.
entities_en: Croatian aces of World War II

sections.subtitle: Pasivnost naroda i neuspjeh lustracije: Posljedice "boljševičkog sotonizma" i političke inercije
sections.screenshot_description: General Glasnović govori o pasivnosti naroda i neuspjehu lustracije.
sections.content: General Glasnović kritizira pasivnost naroda, posebno nisku izlaznost na izbore za Europski parlament (18%), gdje se, kako kaže, "mije koplja na strateškoj razini". Ističe da u Europskom parlamentu sjede "opasni komunisti" koji su se redefinirali kao liberali i demokrati. Voditelj objašnjava da politički akteri i mediji demotiviraju ljude, šaljući poruku da se promjene ne mogu napraviti. General Glasnović to naziva "boljševičkim sotonizmom" i "totalnom inverzijom istine". Naglašava da su Hrvatska i Slovenija jedine postkomunističke zemlje koje nisu provele lustraciju. Objašnjava da je lustracija ne samo kadrovska, već i mentalna, jer ljudi koji su desetljećima živjeli u jednom sustavu ne mogu promijeniti svoj mentalni sklop. Uspoređuje to s Njemačkom, koja je nakon pada Berlinskog zida raspustila vojsku DDR-a i otpustila starije časnike. Smatra da u Hrvatskoj "svi glume", a da se "mentalni sklop transgenetski prenosi" na nasljednike bivšeg sustava.
sections.subtitle_en: Passivity of the people and failure of lustration: Consequences of "Bolshevik satanism" and political inertia
sections.screenshot_description_en: General Glasnović speaks about the passivity of the people and the failure of lustration.
sections.content_en: General Glasnović criticizes the passivity of the people, especially the low turnout in the elections for the European Parliament (18%), where, as he says, "spears are being crossed at a strategic level". He points out that "dangerous communists" sit in the European Parliament, who have redefined themselves as liberals and democrats. The host explains that political actors and media demotivate people, sending the message that changes cannot be made. General Glasnović calls this "Bolshevik Satanism" and "a total inversion of truth". He emphasizes that Croatia and Slovenia are the only post-communist countries that have not carried out lustration. He explains that lustration is not only personnel-related, but also mental, because people who have lived for decades in one system cannot change their mindset. He compares this to Germany, which after the fall of the Berlin Wall disbanded the DDR army and dismissed older officers. He believes that in Croatia "everyone is pretending", and that "the mindset is transgenetically transmitted" to the successors of the former system.

sections.subtitle: Osobna svjedočanstva: Brutalnost "cicarskih plemena" i mučenje Davora Glasnovića
sections.screenshot_description: General Glasnović govori o mučenju svog brata Davora.
sections.content: Voditelj podsjeća na generalovo obiteljsko iskustvo, spominjući mučenje njegovog brata blizanca Davora Glasnovića tijekom zarobljeništva. General Glasnović potresno opisuje kako su Davoru bušili koljena, kako je oslijepio od mučenja i kako su trojica ljudi umrla od batina. Navodi i prisiljavanje zarobljenika da jedu sol na 40 stupnjeva bez vode, što naziva ponašanjem koje ni životinje ne bi pokazale. To pripisuje "cinkarsko-plemenskom" mentalnom sklopu i divljaštvu. Podsjeća da se slični zločini ponavljaju kroz povijest, od Balkanskih ratova do Domovinskog rata, gdje su "susedečka Srpska" činila stravične stvari. Naglašava da su Hrvati živjeli s "glavama" koje nisu dio Europe, te da se multikulturalizam ne može primijeniti na takve poganske običaje poput "lapota" ili pedofilije.
sections.subtitle_en: Personal testimonies: Brutality of "Cicarian tribes" and torture of Davor Glasnović
sections.screenshot_description_en: General Glasnović speaks about the torture of his brother Davor.
sections.content_en: The host recalls the general's family experience, mentioning the torture of his twin brother Davor Glasnović during captivity. General Glasnović movingly describes how Davor's knees were drilled, how he went blind from torture, and how three people died from beatings. He also mentions forcing prisoners to eat salt at 40 degrees without water, which he calls behavior that even animals would not exhibit. He attributes this to a "snitch-tribal" mentality and savagery. He recalls that similar crimes have been repeated throughout history, from the Balkan Wars to the Homeland War, where "neighborly Serbian" entities committed horrific acts. He emphasizes that Croats lived with "heads" that are not part of Europe, and that multiculturalism cannot be applied to such pagan customs as "lapot" or pedophilia.

sections.subtitle: Medijska manipulacija i "poganski" mentalitet: Izazovi razumijevanja istine u suvremenom svijetu
sections.screenshot_description: General Glasnović govori o medijskoj manipulaciji i "poganskom" mentalitetu.
sections.content: General Glasnović kritizira medijsku manipulaciju, koristeći izraz "pita govnara" (10% istine zamotano u 90% laži) za opisivanje propagande. Ističe da mnogi ljudi, čak i obrazovani, ne razumiju "boljševički sotonizam" koji je pobio desetke milijuna ljudi. Navodi primjer Staljina koji je srušio Katedralu Krista Spasitelja u Moskvi i ubio tisuće svećenika, te se pita kako bivši KGB-ovac poput Putina može preko noći postati "produhovljen". Smatra da "malo znanje ubija" jer se ljudi fokusiraju samo na jedan element, što ih čini lakim za zavaravanje. Proširuje raspravu na globalne sukobe, poput rata u Ukrajini, te situaciju u Iranu, Siriji, Libanonu i Iraku, gdje je bio na misiji i svjedočio kršćanskim zajednicama. Kritizira američku propagandu koja te režime prikazuje kao divljake. Postavlja pitanje što je državni terorizam, navodeći genocid nad Armencima. Zaključuje da "tko ima naoružanje, on kroji zakon" i da se narativom manipulira. Upozorava da većina informacija dolazi iz medija koji nisu proveli lustraciju i čiji su vlasnici upitni, te da objektivno novinarstvo ne postoji.
sections.subtitle_en: Media manipulation and "pagan" mentality: Challenges of understanding truth in the contemporary world
sections.screenshot_description_en: General Glasnović speaks about media manipulation and "pagan" mentality.
sections.content_en: General Glasnović criticizes media manipulation, using the expression "shit pie" (10% truth wrapped in 90% lies) to describe propaganda. He points out that many people, even educated ones, do not understand "Bolshevik Satanism" which killed tens of millions of people. He cites the example of Stalin who demolished the Cathedral of Christ the Savior in Moscow and killed thousands of priests, and wonders how a former KGB officer like Putin can become "spiritualized" overnight. He believes that "little knowledge kills" because people focus only on one element, which makes them easy to deceive. He extends the discussion to global conflicts, such as the war in Ukraine, and the situation in Iran, Syria, Lebanon, and Iraq, where he was on a mission and witnessed Christian communities. He criticizes American propaganda that portrays these regimes as savages. He raises the question of what state terrorism is, citing the genocide against the Armenians. He concludes that "whoever has weapons, he makes the law" and that the narrative is manipulated. He warns that most information comes from media that have not undergone lustration and whose owners are questionable, and that objective journalism does not exist.

sections.subtitle: Preispitivanje državnih praznika i zanemarena uloga HVO-a: Borba za istinsko nacionalno sjećanje
sections.screenshot_description: General Glasnović kritizira državne praznike i zanemarenu ulogu HVO-a.
sections.content: Voditelj pita generala Glasnovića o kritici državnih praznika, posebno 22. lipnja. General Glasnović smatra da bi se trebao slaviti povratak hrvatske teritorijalne cjelovitosti, a ne 22. lipnja, te da je za njega "Oluja" pravi praznik. Podsjeća da su nakon "Oluje" uslijedile i druge operacije, poput "Južnog poteza", gdje su hrvatski bojovnici ginuli južno od Banja Luke. Kritizira postojanje "mutant Republike Srpske" i zanemarivanje doprinosa Hrvata iz Bosne i Hercegovine. Ističe da je od gotovo 19.000 poginulih hrvatskih branitelja, pola bilo iz HVO-a, a predsjednik Tuđman je uvijek govorio o "Hrvatskim snagama", ne izdvajajući HVO. Voditelj dodaje da su branitelji izloženi lažima u medijima, što im nanosi dodatnu bol. General Glasnović ponavlja da je 90% zločina u ratu počinjeno od strane Srba i njihovih paravojnih formacija. Zaključuje da je Hrvatska "hibrid države" i da je potrebna akademska i kadrovska lustracija. Voditelj spominje Daliju Orešković koja šovinistički napada Hrvate iz Bosne i Hercegovine, a general Glasnović to naziva "patološkom mržnjom" koja proizlazi iz mentalnog sklopa bivšeg sustava.
sections.subtitle_en: Re-examination of state holidays and the neglected role of HVO: Fight for true national memory
sections.screenshot_description_en: General Glasnović criticizes state holidays and the neglected role of the HVO.
sections.content_en: The host asks General Glasnović about the criticism of state holidays, especially June 22. General Glasnović believes that the return of Croatian territorial integrity should be celebrated, not June 22, and that for him "Oluja" is the true holiday. He reminds that after "Oluja" other operations followed, such as "Južni potez", where Croatian fighters died south of Banja Luka. He criticizes the existence of the "mutant Republika Srpska" and the neglect of the contribution of Croats from Bosnia and Herzegovina. He points out that of almost 19,000 fallen Croatian defenders, half were from the HVO, and President Tuđman always spoke of "Croatian Forces", not singling out the HVO. The host adds that defenders are exposed to lies in the media, which causes them additional pain. General Glasnović reiterates that 90% of war crimes were committed by Serbs and their paramilitary formations. He concludes that Croatia is a "hybrid state" and that academic and personnel lustration is needed. The host mentions Dalija Orešković who chauvinistically attacks Croats from Bosnia and Herzegovina, and General Glasnović calls this "pathological hatred" which stems from the mental framework of the former system.

sections.subtitle: Hrvatska kao "hibridna država": Izborni inženjering i neprovedena lustracija kao prepreke razvoju
sections.screenshot_description: General Glasnović govori o Hrvatskoj kao "hibridnoj državi" i potrebi za lustracijom.
sections.content: General Glasnović ponavlja da je Hrvatska "hibrid države" i da nema funkcionalne kulturne prostore, kritizirajući način na koji HAVC i Grad Zagreb dijele novac za kulturu. Voditelj naglašava da je sadržaj koji se financira javnim novcem često ruglo. General Glasnović ponovno kritizira izborni inženjering, uspoređujući hrvatski sustav s mađarskim, gdje manjine imaju zastupnike, ali bez prava glasa. Voditelj ističe da 12 manjinskih zastupnika u Saboru služi za održavanje HDZ-a i SDP-a na vlasti, te da su se te dvije stranke ujedinile protiv referenduma o promjeni izbornog sustava. General Glasnović prisjeća se svog iskustva u Saboru, kada je glasao po savjesti, čak i ako je to značilo "glasati za onoga tko će napraviti najmanje štete". Spominje konkretne stvari koje je njegova opcija "Nezavisni za Hrvatsku" postigla, poput LNG terminala, koji je riješio energetsku budućnost Hrvatske.
sections.subtitle_en: Croatia as "hybrid state": Electoral engineering and unimplemented lustration as obstacles to development
sections.screenshot_description_en: General Glasnović speaks about Croatia as a "hybrid state" and the need for lustration.
sections.content_en: General Glasnović repeats that Croatia is a "hybrid of a state" and that it has no functional cultural spaces, criticizing the way HAVC and the City of Zagreb distribute money for culture. The host emphasizes that the content financed by public money is often a disgrace. General Glasnović again criticizes electoral engineering, comparing the Croatian system with the Hungarian one, where minorities have representatives, but without the right to vote. The host points out that 12 minority representatives in the Sabor serve to keep HDZ and SDP in power, and that these two parties united against the referendum on changing the electoral system. General Glasnović recalls his experience in the Sabor, when he voted according to his conscience, even if it meant "to vote for the one who will do the least harm". He mentions concrete things that his option "Nezavisni za Hrvatsku" achieved, such as the LNG terminal, which solved Croatia's energy future.

sections.subtitle: Strateška pitanja i nedostatak planiranja: Ovisnost o uvozu i geopolitički izazovi
sections.screenshot_description: General Glasnović govori o strateškim pitanjima hrane, vode i energije.
sections.content: General Glasnović ističe da su hrana, voda i energija strateška pitanja. Kritizira nedostatak planiranja u Hrvatskoj, navodeći primjer uvođenja vojnog roka, o čemu je govorio godinama prije nego što je postao aktualan. Spominje da Hrvatska nema vlastitu proizvodnju streljiva i eksploziva, unatoč izvanrednim uvjetima i kadrovima. Voditelj dodaje da je ovisnost o uvozu hrane veliki problem, o čemu se govori već dvadeset godina, a tek su vanjske okolnosti (poput korone) potaknule svijest o tome. General Glasnović naglašava da su mnoge površine neobrađene zbog neriješenih imovinsko-pravnih odnosa koji vuku korijene iz bivšeg sustava. Upozorava na globalne bitke za pitku vodu, navodeći primjere Kine i Etiopije, te ističe da je voda strateško pitanje. Zaključuje da hrvatski političari nisu sposobni shvatiti važnost strateških pričuva hrane, vode i goriva, te da se djeluje reaktivno, a ne proaktivno.
sections.subtitle_en: Strategic issues and lack of planning: Dependence on imports and geopolitical challenges
sections.screenshot_description_en: General Glasnović speaks about strategic issues of food, water, and energy.
sections.content_en: General Glasnović emphasizes that food, water, and energy are strategic issues. He criticizes the lack of planning in Croatia, citing the example of the introduction of military service, about which he spoke years before it became current. He mentions that Croatia does not have its own production of ammunition and explosives, despite excellent conditions and personnel. The host adds that dependence on food imports is a big problem, which has been discussed for twenty years, and only external circumstances (like corona) have raised awareness of it. General Glasnović emphasizes that many areas are uncultivated due to unresolved property-legal relations that stem from the former system. He warns of global battles for drinking water, citing examples of China and Ethiopia, and points out that water is a strategic issue. He concludes that Croatian politicians are not capable of understanding the importance of strategic reserves of food, water, and fuel, and that actions are reactive, not proactive.

sections.subtitle: Nada u nove generacije: Obrazovanje, istina i proces ozdravljenja društva
sections.screenshot_description: General Glasnović izražava nadu u nove generacije i važnost istine.
sections.content: General Glasnović, unatoč svemu, izražava nadu u nove generacije. Smatra da je država "proces u toku" i da se ne smije očajavati. Naglašava važnost preuzimanja obrazovnih institucija, posebno povijesti i društvenih znanosti, kako bi se "očistili" mentalni sklopovi. Ističe da se "mentalni sklop transgenetski prenosi" i da je potrebno postaviti nacionalno osviještene i obrazovane ljude na ključne pozicije. Spominje Blanku Masković i Rudolfa Horvata kao primjere onih koji su pisali o povijesti. Iako se mnogo toga tiska, problem je što se to ne plasira na prava mjesta i ne prevodi na druge jezike. Zaključuje da je Hrvatska "sakaćena" nakon 1945. godine zbog "aristocida" koji je pobio sve koji su se osjećali Hrvatima, te da se mora ponovno početi ispočetka. Voditelj ističe da su mlade generacije ponosne na branitelje i razumiju svoju povijest, što je znak nade. General Glasnović potvrđuje da je Filozofski fakultet, nekada bastion titoizma, sada mjesto gdje se rade diplomski radovi o istinskoj povijesti.
sections.subtitle_en: Hope in new generations: Education, truth and the process of healing society
sections.screenshot_description_en: General Glasnović expresses hope in new generations and the importance of truth.
sections.content_en: General Glasnović, despite everything, expresses hope in new generations. He believes that the state is "a process in progress" and that one must not despair. He emphasizes the importance of taking over educational institutions, especially history and social sciences, in order to "cleanse" mental frameworks. He points out that "the mental framework is transgenetically transmitted" and that it is necessary to place nationally conscious and educated people in key positions. He mentions Blanka Masković and Rudolf Horvat as examples of those who wrote about history. Although much is printed, the problem is that it is not placed in the right places and not translated into other languages. He concludes that Croatia was "maimed" after 1945 due to "aristocide" which killed all who felt Croatian, and that one must start over again. The host points out that young generations are proud of the veterans and understand their history, which is a sign of hope. General Glasnović confirms that the Faculty of Humanities and Social Sciences, once a bastion of Titoism, is now a place where diploma theses on true history are being written.

sections.subtitle: Generalova osobna lustracija: Čitanje udbaških dosjea u Saboru
sections.screenshot_description: General Glasnović govori o svom iskustvu čitanja UDBA-inih dosjea u Saboru.
sections.content: General Glasnović s dozom humora, ali i gorčine, opisuje svoje osobno iskustvo "lustracije" u Saboru. Kaže da mu je najdraže bilo čitati udbaške dosjee zastupnika, promatrajući kako se "vrte" kada bi im spomenuo kodna imena. Ističe da je to nezamislivo u zemljama poput Litve, Latvije i Estonije, gdje bivši suradnici UDBA-e ne bi mogli sjediti u parlamentu. Naglašava da je još gore što hrvatski porezni obveznici plaćaju te ljude.
sections.subtitle_en: General's personal lustration: Reading UDBA's files in Parliament
sections.screenshot_description_en: General Glasnović speaks about his experience of reading UDBA's files in the Parliament.
sections.content_en: General Glasnović, with a dose of humor but also bitterness, describes his personal experience of "lustration" in the Sabor. He says that his favorite thing was to read the Udba files of representatives, observing how they "spin" when he would mention their code names. He points out that this is unimaginable in countries like Lithuania, Latvia, and Estonia, where former Udba collaborators would not be able to sit in parliament. He emphasizes that it is even worse that Croatian taxpayers pay these people.

sections.subtitle: Povijesne nepravde i zaključak: Borba za istinu kao put prema budućnosti
sections.screenshot_description: General Glasnović i voditelj zaključuju razgovor o povijesnim nepravdama.
sections.content: General Glasnović, iako strpljiv, priznaje da je pesimist u pogledu brzih promjena, ali ističe da je proces u tijeku. Voditelj naglašava da se istina probija, navodeći primjer da se ove godine jasnije govori o tome da Zagreb nije "oslobođen", već je prešao iz jednog diktatorskog sustava u drugi. General Glasnović se prisjeća i ranijih povijesnih nepravda, poput ubojstva Stipe Javora u Srpskoj Mitrovici i napada na domobrane u Zagrebu nakon Prvog svjetskog rata, čiji su nasljednici kasnije sjedili u Saboru. Voditelj zaključuje razgovor zahvaljujući generalu Glasnoviću na njegovom radu na rasvjetljavanju istine. General Glasnović uzvraća zahvalnost, nazivajući voditelja "jedinom svjetlom točkom u medijskom prostoru" i završava s porukom: "Za Hrvatsku i svi za Krista protiv komunista."
sections.subtitle_en: Historical injustices and conclusion: Struggle for truth as a path towards the future
sections.screenshot_description_en: General Glasnović and the host conclude the conversation about historical injustices.
sections.content_en: General Glasnović, although patient, admits that he is a pessimist regarding quick changes, but emphasizes that the process is underway. The host emphasizes that the truth is breaking through, citing the example that this year it is more clearly spoken about that Zagreb was not "liberated", but rather transitioned from one dictatorial system to another. General Glasnović also recalls earlier historical injustices, such as the murder of Stipe Javor in Srpska Mitrovica and the attack on the Home Guard in Zagreb after the First World War, whose successors later sat in the Sabor. The host concludes the conversation by thanking General Glasnović for his work in shedding light on the truth. General Glasnović returns the gratitude, calling the host "the only bright spot in the media space" and concludes with the message: "For Croatia and all for Christ against communists."

iterations.theme_en: Legacy of communism and UDBA in Croatia: History, politics, and struggle for truth
